LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF HOLLY LA LUZ'S MOTION FOR CONDITIONAL CERTIFICATION OF SETTLEMENT CLASS AND PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT. MATTER CALLED, DISCUSSIONS ARE HELD. COUNSEL APPEAR TO DISCUSS THE SCHEDULE FOR THE CONTINUED HEARING AND TO ADDRESS THE ISSUES AS SET FORTH IN THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING. AT THE CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSIONS, THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 1, 2021 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 302. THE SUPPLEMENTAL FILINGS AND REVISED CLASS NOTICE ARE TO BE FILED BY MARCH 29, 2021. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Conditional Certification Of Settlement Class And Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement
Off Calendar Apr-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Patrick Kitchin (415-677-9058; prk@kitchinlegal.com) of Kitchin Legal, APC for Plaintiff, Holly La Luz.
Katie Fox (619-219-5335; kfox@buchalter.com) of Buchalter, APC for Defendant Voxelmaps, Inc.
Leslie Abbott (213.683.6000; leslieabbott@paulhastings.com) of Paul Hastings LLP, for Defendant Tech Mahindra Inc.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF HOLLY LA LUZ's Motion For Conditional Certification Of Settlement Class And Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement:

Having reviewed the supplemental declaration of plaintiff's counsel, the Court grants the unopposed motion for conditional certification of settlement class and preliminary approval of the amended joint stipulation of class action settlement and release.

Final approval hearing is set for August 2, 2021 at 9:30am in Dept 302.

Counsel for Plaintiff to insert the hearing date in the class notice and to submit a proposed order for preliminary approval via email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Conditional Certification Of Settlement Class And Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement
Off Calendar Mar-18-2021 Continued To Apr-01-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Patrick Kitchin, Esq. of Kitchin Law P.C., 415.677.9058, for Plaintiff Holly La Luz;
Atty. Kathryn Fox, Esq. of Buchalter, 619.219.5335, for Defendant Voxelmaps, Inc.;
Atty. Leslie Abbott, Esq. of Paul Hastings LLP, 213.683.6000, for Defendant Tech Mahindra.

Plaintiff HOLLY LA LUZ's Motion For Conditional Certification Of Settlement Class And Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement. Matter called, DISCUSSIONS are held.

Counsel appear to discuss the schedule for the continued hearing and to address the following issues as set forth in the Court's tentative ruling:

1. A proposed settlement of any civil action filed pursuant to the Private Attorneys General Act of 2004 "shall be submitted to [the Labor and Workforce Development Agency] at the same time that it is submitted to the court." (Lab. Code Sec. 2699, subd. (l)(2).) Plaintiff does not show that the proposed settlement was submitted to the LWDA. The Court proposes to continue the hearing on the motion to allow Plaintiff to submit the proposed agreement and the LWDA a reasonable period of time to respond, should it choose to do so.

2. The proposed settlement agreement provides for notice to class members solely by first-class mail ( 27, 52). The Court cannot find that notice to class members by first-class mail only is the best notice practicable. Notice must be provided both by first-class mail and by email, if available. In addition, the settlement agreement should require the settlement administrator to establish a website where class members may access the pleadings, the proposed settlement agreement, and the class notice, as well as information regarding the final fairness hearing.

3. The Court will not approve the provision of the proposed class notice ( 16) which states that any class member who does not object in writing to the proposed settlement will be deemed to have waived his or her objections. Class members may appear and object orally at the final fairness hearing.

4. The reference to the proposed amount of the incentive payment to the named class representative in paragraph 18 of the class notice should include the parenthetical "(subject to approval of the Court)." For counsel's information, the Court is unlikely to approve an incentive payment in excess of $5,000. (See, e.g., Fronda v. Staffmark Holdings, Inc. (N.D. Cal. June 1, 2018), 2018 WL 2463101, at *13-*15 [reducing requested service award for class representative from $10,000 to $5,000].)

At the conclusion of discussions, the matter is continued to April 1, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 302. The supplemental filings and revised class notice are to be filed by March 29, 2021.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
